# @zakkster/lite-headless/calendar

Headless month-view calendar for admin dashboards. Displays events on
a date grid. Not for date input (use createDatePicker for that).

## API

```js
import { createCalendar } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/calendar";

const cal = createCalendar({
    defaultView: new Date(),
    defaultEvents: [
        { id: "1", start: new Date(2026, 5, 10), title: "Meeting", color: "#3b82f6" },
    ],
    defaultSelectedDate: null,
    weekStartsOn: 0,
    onViewChange:  (viewMonth, reason) => {},
    onEventClick:  (event, domEvent)   => {},
    onDateClick:   (date,  domEvent)   => {},
    onSelectedDateChange: (date, reason) => {},
});
```

## Event shape

```js
{
    id:      string,         // required, unique
    start:   Date,            // required
    end:     Date | null,     // optional; null = same-day at start time
    title:   string,          // required
    allDay:  boolean,         // optional
    color:   string | null,   // CSS color string for chip styling
    meta:    {},              // consumer-defined extras
}
```

Multi-day events: an event with start < end returns from `eventsForDay()`
for every day in [start..end]. Consumer detects continuation styling by
comparing event.start to cellDate.startOfDay.

## Reactive accessors

```js
cal.view()             // Date (start of current view month)
cal.events()           // Event[] (sorted by start)
cal.selectedDate()     // Date | null
```

## Mutations

```js
cal.setView(d)               // jumps to start-of-month of d
cal.setSelectedDate(d|null)  // sets the highlighted day
cal.setEvents(arr)           // replace the whole events list
cal.addEvent(ev)             // append (dedup'd by id)
cal.removeEvent(id)
cal.updateEvent(id, partial) // merge partial into existing event
cal.goToPrevMonth()
cal.goToNextMonth()
cal.goToToday()
```

## Queries (non-reactive)

```js
cal.eventsForDay(date)       // Event[] overlapping the day
cal.getEvent(id)             // Event | null
cal.getDaysInView(monthDate?) // 42-cell array {date,inMonth,isToday}
```

## Attach methods

```js
cal.attachRoot(el)                  // host element (role=application)
cal.attachGrid(el)                  // grid container (role=grid)
cal.attachMonthLabel(el)            // reactive month/year label
cal.attachPrevMonth(el)             // click -> goToPrevMonth
cal.attachNextMonth(el)             // click -> goToNextMonth
cal.attachDayCell(el, date)         // role=gridcell, paints state attrs
cal.attachEvent(el, eventId)        // click/keyboard -> onEventClick
```

Each returns an `off()` function. All offs run in LIFO order at `destroy()`.

## Painted attributes

### Day cells (set by attachDayCell)
- `role="gridcell"`
- `data-date="YYYY-M-D"` (unpadded; matches dayKey from datepicker)
- `data-day-of-week="0..6"`
- `data-outside-month` if date is not in current view month
- `data-today` if date is today
- `data-selected` + `aria-selected="true|false"` per selectedDate

### Event elements (set by attachEvent)
- `role="button"` + `tabindex="0"`
- `data-event-id="<id>"`
- `data-event-color="<color>"` if event.color is set
- `data-event-all-day` if event.allDay is true
- `data-event-missing` if the event is no longer in the events list

## Custom element

```html
<lite-calendar view="2026-06-01" week-starts-on="0">
    <header>
        <button data-cal-prev>‹</button>
        <span  data-cal-label></span>
        <button data-cal-next>›</button>
    </header>
    <div data-cal-grid>
        <!-- consumer renders day cells via [data-cal-day][data-date] -->
        <!-- and event chips via [data-cal-event-id] -->
    </div>
</lite-calendar>
```

### Custom element host API

```js
host.view                              // Date (accessor)
host.events                            // Event[] (accessor)
host.selectedDate                      // Date | null (accessor)
host.setView(d) / .setEvents(arr) / .setSelectedDate(d)
host.addEvent(ev) / .removeEvent(id) / .updateEvent(id, partial)
host.goToPrevMonth() / .goToNextMonth() / .goToToday()
host.eventsForDay(date) / .getEvent(id) / .getDaysInView()
host._calendarInstance                 // raw primitive escape hatch
```

### Custom element events
- `viewchange`     `{ detail: { view: Date, reason } }`
- `eventclick`     `{ detail: { event } }`
- `dateclick`      `{ detail: { date } }`
- `selectedchange` `{ detail: { date: Date | null } }`

## Composition

Calendar pairs naturally with:
- `lite-popover` for "click date → show event creation popover"
- `lite-dialog` for "click event → show event detail modal"
- `lite-toast`  for "event saved" notifications
- `lite-skeleton` for loading state while fetching events

Multi-day rendering: query `eventsForDay(cellDate)` per cell, render chip
elements, mark continuations via `event.start < cellDate` /
`event.end > endOfCellDay` to apply CSS for arrow heads / tails.

## Notes

- The primitive doesn't build the grid DOM. Consumer creates cells (a
  6×7 grid of `<button>` or `<div>` is typical) and calls
  `attachDayCell(el, date)`. The wrapper variant auto-discovers cells
  via `[data-cal-day][data-date]`.
- Date helpers are imported from `@zakkster/lite-headless/datepicker`
  to avoid duplication.
- No portaling, no positioner. Headless calendars don't have an overlay
  story; they compose with popover/dialog for that.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
